testcase: How to request a release of Go SDK
query:
title: How to request a release of Go SDK

question: Hi team, could you plz share us the guidance on how to release a Go package whose source code is in azure-sdk-for-go repo?
 
The Release planner seems to not have an option for Go to generate SDK
ground_truth:
You have a couple options. Option 1 is that we can abandon the current plan for you and you can start over. You would need to select the language selection dropdown and select the Go option. Since Go is not a tier-1 data plane language, it is deselected by default. Option 2 is that we can select Go for you on the backend in your existing plan. Let us know how you would like to proceed.

For building a GO data plane package, please refer to this:  https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/blob/main/documentation/developer_setup.md

Also, to avoid having more than one release plan per release, it's recommended to abandon the existing plan before creating a new one.

testcase: Cannot find 'Azure Container Apps' when creating release plan
query:
title: Cannot find 'Azure Container Apps' when creating release plan

question: From release history, my targeted Product Name was 'Azure Container Apps'
```
Release #1645

Summary

Product details
Name
Azure Container Apps
Type
Offering
```
But when I start new one, the production name changes to those 5. Looks they are same production. Which should be selected?
```
Product name

Azure Container Apps - Offering
Azure Container Apps - Offering
Azure Container Apps - Azure Arc - ProductSku
Azure Container Apps - Dedicated - ProductSku
Azure Container Apps – for feature creation purposes only - ProductSku
Azure Container Apps jobs - Feature
```
ground_truth:
from the imagine you are sharing, the first option "Azure Container Apps - Offering".
Also when you select the product you get the link to the service tree instance so you can always double check that is indeed the intended product.
the one highlighted below is the one being used in Release #1645 in your screenshot. To understand better, refer to [Products/Offerings Hierarchy | Service Tree Team Documentation](https://eng.ms/docs/cloud-ai-platform/azure-edge-platform-aep/aep-engineering-systems/engineering-intelligence-standards/service-tree/service-trees-team-doc/stbasics/type-prods)

expected_references.title: Products/Offerings Hierarchy | Service Tree Team Documentation

testcase: Go SDK release completed, but release planner still showing "Pending"
query:
title: Go SDK release completed, but release planner still showing "Pending"

question: Hi Azure SDK Onboarding,
 
We have completed the release for Resource Management - Storagediscovery v.10.0
[armstoragediscovery package - github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/resourcemanager/storagediscover…](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/resourcemanager/storagediscovery/armstoragediscovery)

But the release planner did not update the status after a day.
Release #1906 - GA of Storage Discovery
```
Release SDK for Go
Plan: Release #1906         Target Date: October 2025

Release SDK

⚠️ Once a package is released to the public registry, the action is final and cannot be reversed. Exercise due diligence in validating the package before making it available to ensure that it meets the necessary quality standards and avoids any potential issues post-release.

The Azure SDK is released through an Azure DevOps pipeline. The release pipeline first runs a build stage to compile the SDK, and subsequent approval is required to proceed to the release stage. It is important to approve the release only for the management plane package you intend to release, as these pipelines are shared across all packages within a service.

Release Pipeline Build URL: https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/internal/_build/results?buildId=5408951&view=results

Release Pipeline Build Status: In Progress
```
the linked build https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/internal/_build/results?buildId=5408951&view=results
has completed.
 
Please help checking if anything is off.
Thank you!
ground_truth: Go package name was incorrect in the release planner so the release pipeline did not mark the package as released due to this mismatch. I have fixed the package name in release planner side and marked it as released.

testcase: Question on release planning for new GA version of existing product
query:
title: Question on release planning for new GA version of existing product

question: Hi,
 
I'm the PM for the [BillingRP ARM APIs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/billing/?view=rest-billing-2024-04-01). We currently a stable GA that was released in April 2024 (v2024-04-01) and we are planning to release a new GA version in Jan 2026 (v2026-01-01).
 
The release planner currently shows two releases:
```
Release plans
List of all release plans created for Billing RP

Release plan name    Product name    REST API scope    Release lifecycle
Release#1376    Billing RP - 2024-06-01    Management-Plane    GA
Release#1375    Billing RP - 2024-06-01    Management-Plane    Private Preview
```

I'm assuming I create a new release here for the planned new GA version?
 
Also, when I go to create the new release, I see that the Product name dropdown shows "Billing RP - 2024-04-01 - Offering". Do I create the new release under this even though it references the older version? 

```
Create an SDK release plan
An SDK release plan is guided workflow that contains required tasks for Azure SDK

Product details
Select a product for this release plan.


Product name：
Billing RP - 2024-04-01 - Offering


Product's target lifecycle phase：
```
If I should have a corresponding new offering where should I create it? If there should only be one offering then should I rename the offering to not have the version number in it?
 
Sorry if these are basic questions but I just recently took over these APIs from another PM so I'm a bit unfamiliar with the process.
 
Thanks in advance!
ground_truth: Yes, you should create a new GA release plan. The product dropdown list is populated from what is in service tree for the service. You should get your service tree service-to-product relationships settled before creating the release plan. After you have service tree reflect what you want, you would then use release planner to onboard the product if you will be using a different product than the initial product in the screenshot. TBH I don’t recall seeing a product (I.e. sku, feature, or offering) in service tree with a version in the name before.

testcase: Unable to release Go SDK 1.0.0 - release plan says pull request open, but it is merged.
query:
title: Unable to release Go SDK 1.0.0 - release plan says pull request open, but it is merged.

question: My release plan for `sdk/resourcemanager/durabletask/armdurabletask` v1.0.0 says the pull request status is open, but it is merged.
https://apps.powerapps.com/play/e/ed2ffefd-774d-40dd-ab23-7fff01aeec9f/a/bec76eb9-3c3f-43db-b115-d5084bf488b9?workflow=d8316504-3c98-f011-b4cc-0022480bb79b&tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47
 
Trying to run the release pipeline: https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/internal/_build?definitionId=7389&view=runs
with commit 795397a4bccdcb04e6871d3781bff024f5f79168 is not letting me pick any release artifact, and I cannot run the pipeline.
 
PR is merged: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/pull/25335
Release request: https://github.com/Azure/sdk-release-request/issues/6607
ground_truth: Pull request info was not updated in the SDK release request after it got generated. I have linked Go PR to release plan now. We have automated SDK generation step using copilot now so this won't be an issue going forward.

testcase: Missing package name in the SDK release planner
query:
title: Missing package name in the SDK release planner

question: Hi, we were wondering why we don't see the package name for azure-planetarycomputer in the release planner [Data Plane SDK Release App - Power Apps](https://apps.powerapps.com/play/e/ed2ffefd-774d-40dd-ab23-7fff01aeec9f/a/ac8d05b2-3613-42c4-af44-9fa03d1f6157?workflow=f42f0775-711f-ef11-840b-0022480bd0ae&tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47) ? Is there anything missing?
ground_truth: Package name will be available in SDK release app only after the SDK is merged. It's added by CI pipeline that gets triggered when you merge SDK to main branch.

testcase: Question on Management plane - API readiness and Beta SDK release (NEW)
query:
title: Question on Management plane - API readiness and Beta SDK release (NEW)

question: Hello team,
 
Reaching out on behalf of Azure Data Boundary team, I am unable to close the task Management plane - API readiness and Beta SDK release in the CLC list as the option is disabled.([Service 360](https://vnext.s360.msftcloudes.com/cloudLifecycle/products?global=10:62271e69-c66d-4820-b204-684f5490986a&blade=stage:2~program:All~kpiowner:All~Domains:All~_loc:cloudLifecycle&actionItemId=84715402-4f3c-4dca-b330-f05206abaec5))
 
We have already closed the API readiness and SDK release a while back which is outlined here : [Azure SDK Release Planner - Power Apps](https://apps.powerapps.com/play/e/ed2ffefd-774d-40dd-ab23-7fff01aeec9f/a/821ab569-ae60-420d-8264-d7b5d5ef734c?release-plan-id=d530a575-842e-ef11-840b-000d3a3783ac&tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47)
 
But we are unable to close the task and I wanted to seek some guidance on how could one do it. Thank you!
ground_truth:
the root cause is that between the time you completed the release plan and sought to get your CLC KPI approved, we released automation where when a release plan is completed we automatically approve the KPI without you having to submit it manually.
I will need to manually approve the KPI.

query:
title: Release plan target dates

question: Hi, I wish to create release plans for our first public preview release and have approximate dates for SDK and API readiness, however the dates may change based on business needs or for technical reasons that we don't yet know. Can we modify the dates after creating the plan? I don't see a way to modify dates in older release plans we have, but I might have missed it.
 
And what would happen if we didn't change it in the release plan but anyways deviated from the target dates?
 
Thanks!
ground_truth:
Regarding 'Can we modify the dates after creating the plan?'
Looks like from the UI you can't modify the API spec date, but you can modify the SDK release date, which at the end is the most important one for us. You can do this in the `Summary` view of the Release plan.

Regarding 'And what would happen if we didn't change it in the release plan but anyways deviated from the target dates?'
We only monitor the SDK release date. If you miss the date you will get an email from the PM team asking about the release.

testcase: Unable to onboard new feature product in our service
query:
title: Unable to onboard new feature product in our service

question: Hi,
I'm trying to onboard a new feature to our service. I've already added the feature to the same service tree, but it's still not showing up in the Azure SDK release planner. I checked the onboarding documentation, which says to fill out the form in Onboard CPEX product, but I can't seem to locate it. Does anyone have any suggestions for onboarding a new feature in the same service?
ground_truth:
Feature [Service Tree | Products | edge marketplace](https://microsoftservicetree.com/products/55b6d919-2147-4494-9893-36105f7a3d54) was added yesterday in service tree. Release planner syncs data with the service tree daily so this new feature was not available in the release planner earlier.  Release planner completed today's data  sync and you should be able to select Edge marketplace in release planner now.
```
Product details
Select your offering, feature, or SKU.

Product name
Choose one
    disconnected operations - ProductSku
    edge marketplace - Feature
```

testcase: our release planner has older package namespaces which were changed during the discussion
query:
title: our release planner has older package namespaces which were changed during the discussion

question: our release planner has older package namespaces which were changed during the discussion, but I'm unable to update them since the new namespaces are not in the prepopulated list. How do we update these?
ground_truth:
Release planner shows only existing list of packages when the product has an existing package. This is a known limitation in the release planner. I have marked in the release planner that product has a new package so you can type the package name.

```
Is this the first SDK release of this package?
GitHub issue for namespace approval:    URL of GitHub issue for namespace approval
Namespaces need to be approved prior to their SDK release. Click here for more details about namespace approval.

Provide package namespace for each language
.NET    Azure.ResourceManager
Python    azure-mgmt-managementgroups
JavaScript    @azure/arm-managementgroups
Java    azure-resourcemanager-managementgroups
Go    sdk/resourcemanager/managementgroups/armmanageme...
```

testcase: Urgent help needed for GA release
query:
title: Urgent help needed for GA release

question: Our release plan is not taking the latest namespace which we have setup in the code. How to setup the namespace for the release plan?
Its taking Azure.ResourceManager.trustedsigning, it should pick Azure.ResourceManager.ArtifactSigning which has been setup in the code
The namespace review was done for private preview few years back. For GA we are going ahead with a different namespace.
ground_truth:
Release planner shows only existing package names when product already has released a package for it. This is a known limitation. I have marked it as new package so you can type in the name in pre-requisite tab. You will need to provide package approval issue link. 
 
You can also use azsdk agent from vscode to update package names in the release plan. For more details: aka.ms/azsdk/agent

testcase: Question about Arch Tech Board information meeting time
query:
title: Question about Arch Tech Board information meeting time

question: Hi everyone, I’m currently working on getting a new TypeSpec project merged into the azure-rest-api-specs repo and have been following the CI guidance.
 
It looks like the next step might be to schedule an informational meeting with the Arch Tech Board through the automated system. However, I’m not entirely sure if that applies in our case.
 
We’re not introducing a brand new service. This is an additional layer on top of an existing service, but it can be used independently, so we created a separate TypeSpec for it.

Could someone help confirm whether we still need to schedule the informational meeting in this scenario? Thanks!

PR link: [Add Web PubSub Chat project by Y-Sindo · Pull Request #41187 · Azure/azure-rest-api-specs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41187)
ground_truth:
per our latest folder structure guidelines:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/wiki/Azure-Service-Versioning-Guideline

you actually are adding a new "service", named specification/webpubsub/data-plane/WebPubSubChat
 
importantly, this new service WebPubSubChat has no relation to its existing sibling service WebPubSub
 
whether all this is the correct design, is a good thing to discuss with the arch board

testcase: Feature not showing up in Azure SDK Release Planner "Product name" dropdown
query:
title: Feature not showing up in Azure SDK Release Planner "Product name" dropdown

question: I am trying to run through the Azure SDK Release Planner "Onboard your product" flow to onboard two APIs that already live in the azure-rest-api-specs  and azure-rest-api-specs-pr repos, but have seemingly not been onboarded to this release planner (our team took ownership of these RPs recently, and we are trying to bring the APIs to GA), Microsoft.PortalServices/compilefile and Microsoft.Portal/dashboards. 
 
We created features for these and connected them to the Portal Framework Service Tree around 2 days ago, but the Azure SDK Release Planner still cannot find them. Am I missing something in the ServiceTree metadata?
ground_truth:
Can you please select Azure Portal Services Resource Provider as the service in release planner app? 
Azure Portal Declarative Framework feature is listed under Portal services resource provider in service tree and release planner.
 
Similarly Azure Portal Dashboards is under service Azure Portal Framework (IbizaFx)

testcase: Need to restart or edit Release but do not want to have SDKs regenerated
query:
title: Need to restart or edit Release but do not want to have SDKs regenerated

question: I initiated this release to onboard Horizon DB to the SDKs. However, a couple of weeks ago the product name changed—from Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Orion DB – Offering to Azure HorizonDB – ProductSku.
I am not seeing a way to edit the release to point to a different product, so we are considering restarting the release. My concern is that since the release is already in progress, with some SDKs published and another still under review, closing and restarting the release could disrupt our current progress. Would restarting impact our position in the pipeline?
Additionally, is there a way to associate a new release with the existing SDK PRs to avoid triggering a new set of SDK generations?
ground_truth: You should be good to go. Keep working the same release plan. We pull name changes directly from Service Tree, so the new name is already reflected in our ADO SDK source of truth. And FYI release planner will be EOL'd soon, so you should start using the SDK Agent going forward. Release plans will continue, but the Release Planner app will not.
